data.title: Huawei unveils one million yuan EV to crack China luxury market
data.description:
(May 30): Huawei Technologies Co and its automaking partners are pushing further into the luxury end of China’s car market with the launch of the Maextro S800, a sedan with a price tag exceeding the one million yuan (RM589,197) mark.

The smartphone maker and Anhui Jianghuai Automobile Group Corp, or JAC, unveiled the vehicle in Shenzhen on Friday. Priced at 1.02 million yuan for its most premium version, the car is designed to go up against top-end models from Mercedes-Benz Group AG and Volkswagen AG’s Bentley.

The Maextro S800 offers options seen in higher-end competitor models including a crystal shimmer ceiling — pioneered by Rolls-Royce — that’s meant to look like a starry night sky. The car comes with a triple-screen dashboard and more than 30 sensors that help enable Huawei’s advanced driver-assistance technology.

“The Chinese auto industry is advancing towards the top of the world’s value chain,” Richard Yu, Huawei’s consumer business group head, said during the glitzy event that featured an opera performance.

Huawei’s venture into the luxury segment with Seres Group Co produced the Aito brand which were the best sellers in the 500,000 yuan and above category in China last year.

Huawei’s venture into the luxury segment comes after tasting initial success with another collaboration — the Aito brand produced together with Seres Group Co. Those vehicles were the best sellers in the 500,000 yuan and above category in China last year, beating models from BMW AG and Mercedes.

Huawei and its partners are now betting that Chinese luxury consumers are increasingly trading in their gas guzzlers for battery-powered vehicles.

The chairman for JAC, Xiang Xingchu, and the founder of battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology Co Ltd, Robin Zeng, also presented on stage, with Zeng emphasising the safety and convenience of the Maextro’s fast-charging battery.

Previewing the car in February, Yu played a video that benchmarked it against the Maybach S680, which can cost over three million yuan. The Maextro’s most basic version starts from 708,000 yuan. 

BYD, China’s No 1 car brand, recently slashed the prices of some of its models by as much as 34%, forcing a slew of other automakers to follow suit. It has also launched a million-yuan EV range called Yangwang, which includes a Ferrari-like sports car, the U9, that starts from 1.7 million yuan and the luxury sport utility vehicle, the U8, that sells for around 1.1 million yuan.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Wall Street falls as Trump says China violated tariff terms
data.description:
(May 30): Wall Street's main indexes fell on Friday as US President Donald Trump accused China of violating a tariff agreement, ramping up tensions in a trade war that has roiled global markets and businesses.

Trump's post on his Truth Social platform did not specify how China had violated the agreement made in Geneva, Switzerland, and what action he would take against Beijing. "So much for being Mr. NICE GUY!," he said.

In their Geneva talks, both sides had agreed the US would drop levies on Chinese imports from 145% to 30% during a 90-day negotiation period and China would cut duties from 125% to 10%.

"We went from a place where it felt as though investors had a pretty good handle on the direction of trade and tariffs and now that picture has become very confusing," said Art Hogan, chief market strategist at B Riley Wealth.

Helping stem losses, data showed US consumer spending increased marginally in April, with the year-on-year reading increasing to 2.1% after advancing 2.3% in March. The Fed tracks the PCE price measures for its 2% inflation target.

Traders continued to bet that the US central bank will cut its target for short-term borrowing costs in September.

At 09:59am ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 49.27 points, or 0.12%, to 42,166.46, the S&P 500 lost 27.12 points, or 0.46%, to 5,885.05 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 155.75 points, or 0.81%, to 19,020.13.

Most megacap and growth stocks fell, with Nvidia down 2.4%.

Nine of the 11 major S&P 500 sub-sectors fell, with energy and information technology declining the most.

Despite the losses, the Nasdaq is on pace for its best month since November 2023, while the benchmark S&P 500 is on track for its biggest monthly gain since November 2024. The Dow is also set to notch a near 3.5% advance for May.

It has been a volatile month for stocks as Trump's on-and-off trade moves kept investors on edge, though his softening stance on tariffs had helped the S&P 500 rebound from its April lows. The benchmark is now about 4% lower than its all-time high hit in February.

Hopes of more deals between the US and major trading partners, along with upbeat earnings and tame inflation data, had also driven the gains in equities.

US equities had initially rallied in the previous session, after the Court of International Trade ruled late on Wednesday to effectively block most levies imposed since January, without addressing some industry-specific tariffs.

However, a federal appeals court on Thursday temporarily reinstated most of the tariffs and ordered the plaintiffs in the cases to respond by June 5 and the administration by June 9.

Among other big movers on the day, Ulta Beauty jumped 15% after the cosmetics retailer raised its annual profit forecast after beating quarterly results.

Shares of drugmaker Regeneron dropped more than 18% after its experimental drug for patients with a type of lung condition commonly called "smoker's lung" failed a late-stage trial, although it succeeded in another.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 2.33-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 2.66-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted six new 52-week highs and two new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 24 new highs and 40 new lows.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Alliance Bank expects OPR to remain at 3% amid stable economic growth
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Alliance Bank Bhd (KL:ABMB) expects Bank Negara Malaysia to maintain the overnight policy rate (OPR) at 3% for the remainder of this year, citing strong gross domestic product (GDP) figures, well-managed inflation, and currency strength

“Our current house view is that we do expect [the] OPR to remain at 3% for various reasons," said Alliance Bank group CEO Kellee Kam on Friday.

"One is inflation, which we believe is fairly well controlled. Another is that GDP grew by 4.4% in the first quarter this year, which is still growing pretty strongly. The number three reason is that our currency performance has also been pretty strong relative to the dollar, even relative to some of our regional peers," said Kam.

He was speaking to the media after the bank’s extraordinary meeting, where shareholders approved a proposed right issue,

Shareholders holding 57.28% of voting shares, or 886.79 million shares, voted in favour of the resolution, according to the group's bourse filing on Friday.

Rights issue price fixed at RM3.33

Alliance Bank has fixed the rights issue price at RM3.33 apiece on an entitlement basis of two rights shares for every 17 existing shares held.

The issue price represents a discount of 23% compared to the stock's closing price of RM4.33 on Friday.

Alliance Bank is expected to raise RM606.5 million from the cash call exercise, according to a back-of-the-envelope calculation.

Kam said the rights issue is aimed at supporting its future growth, noting that the bank has outpaced the industry by growing loans at 12% in FY2024, compared to the market growth rate of between 5% and 6%.

Going forward, Alliance Bank has targeted a loan growth of between 8% and 10% in the financial year ending March 31, 2026 (FY2026).

“We want to position ourselves with enough capital to continue that growth,” he said. “It is actually the opposite of tougher times — we want to not slow down because we believe there’s an opportunity for Alliance Bank to continue that pace of growth.”

He added that Malaysia's banking sector remains accommodative for growth, supported by strong GDP figures, sufficient liquidity, and fiscal and monetary levers.

Meanwhile, Kam said Alliance Bank has not been informed of any potential changes to its shareholding structure, amid speculation that its largest shareholder, Vertical Theme Sdn Bhd’s (VT), may divest its stake.

According to a Bloomberg report on Jan 16, VT was planning to seek approval from BNM to start talks about selling its stake in Alliance Bank. The newswire, citing people familiar with the development, said VT was considering selling the roughly 29% stake to Singapore-based lender DBS Group Holdinhgs Ltd.

VT is 49% held by Duxton Investments Pte Ltd, which is ultimately owned by Temasek. The other 51% in VT is held by Langkah Bahagia Sdn Bhd, a private company owned by Singapore-based hotel and property tycoon Ong Beng Seng, private equity RRJ Capital founder Richard Ong Tiong Sing and corporate adviser Seow Lun Hoo. All three are Malaysians.

Beng Seng’s interest in the bank, through VT, has come into the spotlight because of his corruption probe in Singapore. He faces two charges in relation to former Singapore transport minister S Iswaran, who is currently serving time in jail for his offences.

Kam also confirmed that Beng Seng remains a shareholder of Alliance Bank via VT.

“As far as I am aware, he is still a shareholder of Alliance Bank,” Kam said, when asked about the current shareholder structure.

Other major shareholders in Alliance Bank include the Employees Provident Fund with a 9.3% stake, and Global Success Network Sdn Bhd with a 4.98% stake. Global Success is the investment vehicle of Lee Thiam Wah, founder of retail chain 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd (KL:99SMART), Bloomberg data showed.

The bank's net profit rose 8.7% to RM750.73 million for the financial year ended March 31, 2025, from RM690.48 million in the previous year. Revenue climbed 12.3% to RM2.27 billion from RM2.02 billion.

Alliance Bank shares closed down four sen or 0.9% at RM4.33, bringing the group a market capitalisation of RM6.7 billion. The stock has fallen 15% since the announcement of the rights issue in March.

Read also:
Cover Story: Alliance Bank needs to do more for a better premium valuation, say analysts
Cover Story: Can Alliance Bank keep up its strong growth momentum?
Alliance Bank proposes renounceable rights issue of shares to raise RM600m
All eyes on Vertical Theme’s next move in Alliance Bank

 

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Loss-making LYC Healthcare falls under GN3 status
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): LYC Healthcare Bhd (KL:LYC) has been classified as a Guidance Note 3 (GN3) company after its shareholders’ equity fell below 25% of its issued share capital, based on unaudited results for the financial year ended March 31, 2025 (FY2025).

GN3 status applies to financially distressed companies listed on Bursa Malaysia’s ACE Market, similar to Practice Note 17 (PN17) classification on the Main Market.

The loss-making healthcare group — which operates confinement homes, childcare centres, senior living homes and family clinics — said it is formulating a regularisation plan to address the GN3 classification.

The company has 12 months to submit its plan and secure Bursa Malaysia’s approval. If it fails to do so, its securities may be suspended and the company will be delisted.

For the fourth quarter ended March 31 (4QFY2025), LYC Healthcare narrowed its net loss to RM8.7 million from RM8.91 million a year earlier amid lower operating costs.

Quarterly revenue fell 9.6% to RM31.82 million from RM35.22 million, mainly due to weaker contributions from its Malaysian operations.

For the full year, the group narrowed its net loss to RM17.39 million from RM19.16 million in FY2024, as revenue rose 17.9% to RM153.53 million. LYC has been loss-making since 2012.

Separately, the group announced the appointment of Lee Ai Vi, its group accountant, as new chief financial officer.

Looking ahead, LYC said it will continue to pursue expansion both locally and abroad, focusing on growing its clinical and nutraceutical segments while optimising the performance of its subsidiaries.

“The group remains confident in its longer-term growth by improving our positioning in the growing healthcare markets and we will continue delivering encouraging performance in coming years through consolidation and execution of our committed business plans,” it added.

Shares of LYC Healthcare closed down half a sen or 8.33% to 5.5 sen on Friday, valuing the group at RM44.4 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Aumas Resources, formerly Bahvest, secures injunction prohibiting ex-CEO from interfering with storage facility
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Aumas Resources Bhd (KL:AUMAS), the Sabah-based gold miner formerly known as Bahvest Resources Bhd, announced on Friday that it has secured a court injunction prohibiting its former CEO Datuk Lo Fui Ming from interfering with the group’s tailing storage facility (TSF).

The ex-parte injunction bars Lo and Southsea Gold Sdn Bhd, which is 75%-owned by Lo, from obstructing rectification and modification works on the TSF’s perimeter drainage system. The breach in the TSF occurred due to the diversion of a significant volume of rainwater into the tailing dam.

The High Court order also ensures unhindered access for all vehicles, equipment and personnel involved in the repair works, according to Aumas’ filing with Bursa Malaysia.

The court has scheduled an inter-partes hearing of the injunction application for June 12.

Aumas' mining operations require the storage of diesel and chemicals in protective casings, appropriate bunkers and enclosures to prevent spillage. These bunkers provide layers of protection against diesel and chemical spillages that could contaminate soil and flow into water catchment areas and sources.

Oil sumps are constructed to filter diesel waste before diverting it into the constructed drain leading to the TSF, according to the group's annual report.

Lo, who is the founder of Aumas, resigned mid-2023 due to multiple challenges, including boardroom tussles, an authorities’ raid and halted mining production.

He was one of three directors — including his son and executive director Lo Tek Yong, and non-executive chairman Datuk Seri Dr Md Kamal Bilal — who stepped down ahead of the group's extraordinary general meeting, following shareholders’ intention to remove them.

Aumas’ shares closed down one sen or 1.45% at 68 sen on Friday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM1.26 billion.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Proton appoints Ainol Azmil as covering deputy CEO
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Proton Holdings Bhd has announced the appointment of Ainol Azmil as covering deputy chief executive officer (CEO), effective June 10. 

He succeeds Roslan Abdullah, who will be stepping down after five years as deputy CEO.

In a statement issued on Friday, the national carmaker said Ainol will report directly to Proton’s chief executive officer, Dr Li Chunrong. 

In addition to his new responsibilities, Ainol will continue to oversee his existing portfolio in corporate strategy and group technical procurement.

During Roslan’s tenure, he played a key role in strengthening the brand’s position in Malaysia and oversaw Proton’s entry into the electric vehicle (EV) segment, marked by the introduction of the Proton e.MAS 7.

“Proton welcomes Ainol Azmil to the role of covering deputy chief executive officer. We are confident in his ability to take on this temporary appointment.

“At the same time, Proton would like to convey utmost gratitude to Roslan Abdullah for his contribution to the company and we wish him well in his future undertakings,” said Li.

Proton is 50.1%-owned by DRB-Hicom Bhd (KL:DRBHCOM).

DRB-Hicom’s shares closed half a sen or 0.6% lower at 77.5 sen on Friday, valuing the group at RM1.50 billion. Year to date, the stock has retreated 30.18%.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Tropicana group CEO Mark Victor redesignated as non-executive director
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Tropicana Corp Bhd (KL:TROP) has redesignated its group chief executive officer Datuk Mark Victor Rozario as a non-independent non-executive director.

The changes will be effective on June 1, 2025, according to the property developer's filing with the exchange on Friday.

Rozario, 60, took on the role as group CEO, along with the non-independent executive director position on Dec 3 last year.

He also sits on the board of Petronas Gas Bhd (KL:PETGAS) as an independent non-executive director. Rozario previously served as CEO of General Electric International Inc, Agensi Inovasi Malaysia, Country Heights Holdings Bhd (KL:CHHB) and Adventa Bhd (KL:ADVENTA).

On Thursday, Tropicana posted a net profit of RM1.32 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), lifted by a tax credit of RM6.63 million. The group has remained profitable for two consecutive quarters since its last net loss of RM454.9 million in 3QFY2024.

Revenue for the quarter fell 10.61% to RM260.36 million from RM291.28 million in 1QFY2024, attributed to lower recurring income following the completion of several investment property disposals.

Tropicana shares settled down two sen or 1.67% to RM1.18 on Friday, valuing the group at RM2.97 billion.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Capital A returns to black in 1Q on forex gain
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Capital A Bhd (KL:CAPITALA) swung back to profitability with a net profit of RM689.57 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), mainly due to foreign exchange gain of RM74.1 million in the current quarter, alongside growth in its travel, logistics, maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) services and other travel related businesses.

The low-cost carrier's turnaround followed a net loss of RM91.55 million posted a year earlier and RM1.57 billion in 4QFY2024.

As a result, the carrier posted an earnings per share of 15.9 sen in 1QFY2025 compared with a loss per share of 2.2 sen in 1QFY2024.

Revenue for the quarter rose 15.2% to RM414.52 million in 1QFY2025 from RM359.76 million in 1QFY2024, driven by growth from its logistics, MRO services, and digital businesses.

No dividend was declared during the current quarter. The last dividend declared by Capital A was 90 sen per share for FY2019.

Its aviation segment, which is being classified under discontinuing operations due to an ongoing disposal to its sister company AirAsia X Bhd (KL:AAX), posted a net profit of RM880.88 million on revenue of RM4.91 billion in 1QFY2025, as fuel cost declines and cost optimisation lifted margins.

“We’ve set ambitious but realistic internal targets for 2025, and I’m pleased to say we remain on track,” Capital A chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes said in a statement on Friday.

“Breakeven is targeted towards the later part of the year as BigPay (fintech) leverages on the AirAsia ecosystem as well as key partnerships. I’m confident 2025 will be a defining year for Capital A,” he added.

The aviation business, although classified under discontinuing operations, remains poised for recovery as AirAsia Aviation Group CEO Bo Lingam expects a stronger momentum in the second half of 2025, driven by seasonal demand, a favourable fuel environment, and stronger regional currencies.

“With plans to reactivate 20 aircraft and add a net of two new aircraft, we are positioning to scale capacity as we undertake demand-led network optimisation aimed at boosting frequency and connectivity across the Aviation Group,” he said.

The group targets carrying 70 million passengers in FY2025, with 16.17 million achieved in 1QFY2025. While full fleet reactivation has been delayed to July 2025 due to supply chain issues, new aircraft inductions are expected to mitigate this.

In a separate filing with Bursa Malaysia, Capital A said the carrier and AAX have mutually agreed to further extend the cut-off dates involving the proposed disposals of AirAsia Aviation Group Ltd and AirAsia Bhd by another two months from May 31 to July 31.

"This is to allow additional time for the company and AAX to, among others, obtain the approval and/or consent of the relevant authorities, financiers/lenders and/or third parties as well as for AAX to finalise the definitive terms with the identified investors in relation to AAX’s proposed pre-completion private placement exercise," it added.

Capital A shares closed down two sen or 2.3% at 85 sen on Friday, valuing the group at RM3.69 billion. The stock has fallen 16.67% year to date.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: Far East 1Q net profit rises 67% on higher palm oil prices, and FFB processing volumes
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Far East Holdings Bhd (KL:FAREAST) reported a 67% increase in net profit for the first quarter, supported by higher palm oil prices and improved fresh fruit bunches (FFB) processing volumes.

The net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) rose to RM47.93 million from RM28.7 million a year earlier, according to the company's filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday. Earnings per share increased to 8.07 sen from 4.83 sen.

Quarterly revenue increased 41.53% to RM205.94 million from RM145.51 million in 1QFY2024.  

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

The group said that the improved financial performance was primarily driven by higher average prices of crude palm oil and palm kernel, which rose by 18% and 63% year-on-year to RM4,730 and RM3,632 per tonne, respectively.

This was further supported by a 36% increase in FFB processed by the group’s palm oil mills amounting to an additional 40,495 tonnes.

Looking ahead, Far East Holdings noted that barring any unforeseen developments, CPO and palm kernel prices are expected to remain stable for the FY2025.

However, FFB production is projected to be lower compared to FY2024, which could temper overall performance. The group added that ongoing weather-related disruptions may continue to pose operational challenges.

Far East shares closed one sen or 0.3% lower at RM3.59 on Friday, valuing the group at RM2.13 billion. Year to date, the stock has retreated 1.64%.
 

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Minority shareholders seek court order to access records for Kumpulan Jetson EGM to remove board
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): A group of Kumpulan Jetson Bhd’s (KL:JETSON) minority shareholders, who were blocked from holding an extraordinary general meeting (EGM) to remove the entire board, have asked the court to compel the company to give them access to its shareholder records so they can hold the meeting.

According to a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the shareholders, who claim to collectively own 10.68% of the company, want access to the shareholder list as of May 19. They have asked the High Court to allow the EGM to be held on June 18 or another date set by the court to discuss the issues they raised in their April 23 notice.

The five minority shareholders making the requisition are Yudishthiran Doraisamy (4.05%), Dhayalini PG Doraisamy (4.01%), PG Doraisamy P Gopal (0.79%), Wong Hong Yuk (1.21%) and Teoh Cheng Fang (0.62%).

The group is also asking the court to stop the company from making any changes to the board, shares, or unusual business deals until after the EGM is held.

They want the company to pay for the legal costs and any other court orders the judge thinks are necessary. Kumpulan Jetson said the court has fixed the above matter for hearing on June 9.

This latest development follows Kumpulan Jetson’s board decision to hold off calling for the requisitioned EGM, pending the outcome of a legal suit filed by three minority shareholders challenging the validity of the notice dated April 23.

The board said the decision was made after consulting lawyer Eric Tan from Messrs Ong Kok Bin & Co, because continuing with the requisition might risk the company and its board being held in contempt of court.

Three shareholders — Ting Hoong Yi (1.35%), Ma Xiao Xiang (1.87%) and Tang Yan (1.64%) — have filed a lawsuit to cancel the April 23 requisition notice. They also want the court to stop Kumpulan Jetson from acting on the requisition until the legal case is fully resolved.

The April 23 requisition sought to remove all existing directors, including chairman Tunku Nooruddin Tunku Shahabuddin, executive director Edward Ng Kah Jiun, and non-executive directors Louise Paul Joseph Paul, Hong Cheong Liang and Goh Rui Yee.

In their place, the requisitioners proposed appointing Hong Yuk, Cheng Fang, Ilham Abadi Idris, Looi Mun Fei and Ranjit Singh Karter Singh as new directors.

Kumpulan Jetson recorded a net loss of RM17.61 million for the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025), with a quarterly revenue of RM42.59 million. For FY2025, the company reported a net loss of RM22.15 million on total revenue of RM197.32 million.

No comparative data is available, as the company changed its financial year-end from Dec 31 to March 31 on Jan 26, 2024.

Kumpulan Jetson shares closed unchanged at 19 sen, valuing the group at RM79.2 million. 

Read also:
Kumpulan Jetson puts shareholders’ requisition for EGM on hold pending legal outcome
Kumpulan Jetson gets writ of summons to void shareholders' requisition notice
Kumpulan Jetson’s board faces third removal attempt since December 2023

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: AME Elite’s 4Q net profit rises 40% on strong construction revenue
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): AME Elite Consortium Bhd (KL:AME) on Friday reported a 40.11% increase in net profit for its fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025), mainly driven by higher revenue from its construction services segment.

Net profit increased to RM36.54 million from RM26.08 million a year earlier, according to the Johor-based industrial property developer's exchange filing.

Quarterly revenue rose 36.33% to RM115.64 million from RM84.82 million.

Revenue from its construction services segment tripled to RM35.83 million from RM11.53 million in 4QFY2024, amid progress in ongoing project completions.

All other segments recorded revenue growth except for the engineering services segment, which declined due to progression in the completion stage of ongoing engineering projects.

The company declared a second interim dividend of three sen per share, down from four sen last year, bringing the total dividend for the year to six sen. The dividend will be paid on July 4.

For the full financial year, AME Elite’s net profit dipped 1.09% to RM92.09 million from RM93.10 million in FY2024, mainly due to lower contributions from the property development segment and a higher share of losses from equity-accounted joint ventures. Annual revenue declined 15.10% to RM608.57 million from RM716.88 million previously.

The company said it expects to deliver a satisfactory financial performance for FY2026.

It noted that Johor's industrial property sector is expected to stay active, driven by major infrastructure projects like the Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS, the potential revival of the KL-Singapore HSR and the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone.

The company said it remains focused on developing i-TechValley at the SILC Industrial Park in Johor, which is expected to boost profitability.

Additionally, AME Elite' joint venture with Majestic Gen Sdn Bhd, Suling Hill Development Sdn Bhd, will lead the Northern TechValley @ BKE project in Penang.

The planned land acquisition from Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd (KL:KLK), set for completion in FY2026, will further support the company’s long-term growth strategy, the company added.

AME Elite’s shares closed up one sen or 0.6% to RM1.61 on Friday, valuing the group at RM1.03 billion. Year to date, the stock has declined 5.29%.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Weak sales drag BAT Malaysia’s 1Q profits, dividends to 20-year low
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): British American Tobacco (Malaysia) Bhd (KL:BAT) saw its net profit fall to a 20-year low, weighed down by lower sales volume due to seasonal factors and the early start of the Ramadan fasting period.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), dropped over 22% to RM23.3 million from RM30 million a year earlier, on the back of a 22% decline in revenue to RM322 million from RM412 million.

This marks the tobacco group's weakest bottom-line and top-line performance in at least two decades.

BAT Malaysia, in its bourse filing, said although overall market demand dropped due to the early Ramadhan fasting period, Dunhill’s market share grew by 0.7%, keeping it Malaysia’s top brand. However, other premium and value brands saw declines, causing the group’s total market share to fall slightly by 0.6%.

The group’s sales volume dropped 20.6% compared to last year, leading to the lower revenue. Despite this, gross profit margin improved by 2.9%, thanks to better product mix.

Despite the slump, BAT Malaysia declared a first interim dividend of 7.5 sen per share — its lowest in at least 20 years — amounting to RM21.5 million, compared with 10 sen per share declared in 1QFY2024. The dividend is payable on July 3.

On a quarter-on-quarter basis, net profit halved from RM49 million, while revenue also fell sharply from RM653 million.

In a separate statement, BAT Malaysia managing director Nedal Salem said the weak performance reflects the challenging operating environment, including persistent issues with the tobacco black market and softening demand.

However, he expressed confidence in the group’s outlook for the remainder of the financial year, as BAT Malaysia continues to grow Dunhill’s leadership in Malaysia along with other brands in the aspirational premium and value-for-money segments.

As the new Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 is rolled out, BAT Malaysia said it is fully prepared for the changes.

With new vape rules starting on Oct 1, 2025, the company will phase out its Vuse products in the third quarter and review the regulations before planning future launches, while focusing on its traditional cigarette business.

Shares in BAT Malaysia fell 25 sen or 4.1% to close at RM5.85 on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.67 billion. Year to date, the counter has fallen over 21.6%.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Aurelius Technologies posts modest 2% growth in 1Q profit, raises dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Electronic manufacturing services provider Aurelius Technologies Bhd (KL:ATECH) reported a 2.35% rise in net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), primarily due to performance in the communication and Internet of Things (IoT) segment, which accounted for 84.2% of total revenue. 

The group posted a net profit of RM16.10 million versus RM15.73 million in the same quarter last year, according to its filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday. Earnings per share fell to 3.71 sen from four sen in 1QFY2024. 

The company declared a first interim single-tier dividend of 3.6 sen per share, up from 2.7 sen per share a year prior, with payment scheduled for July 15. 

Quarterly revenue rose 17.62% to RM147.85 million from RM125.70 million a year earlier. The increase was largely driven by communication and IoT products, which contributed the majority share of revenue. This was followed by electronic devices contributing 10.3% and semiconductor components accounting for 5.5%. 

Looking forward, Aurelius Technologies said it is actively exploring new investment opportunities arising from supply chain shifts and trade diversification into Malaysia. 

The group emphasised a prudent and strategic approach, focusing on strengthening its technological infrastructure to support future growth. These efforts are aligned with its expansion plans targeting the automotive, fintech and artificial intelligence sectors. 

In addition, the company is enhancing process automation and expanding its surface mount technology infrastructure, which currently includes 16 lines, in line with rising order book demand.

Aurelius shares closed one sen or 0.3% lower at RM3.29 on Friday, valuing the group at RM1.43 billion. Year to date, the stock has retreated 6%.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: RGB reports 44% drop in 1Q net profit on softer sales
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Electronic gaming machines distributor RGB International Bhd (KL:RGB) has reported a 44.14% year-on-year decline in net profit for the first quarter, dragged by lower sales volume.

The net profit fell to RM12.39 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025 from RM22.18 million previously, according to a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday. Earnings per share dropped to 0.8 sen from 1.44 sen.

Quarterly revenue stood at RM73.57 million, down 64.99% from RM210.11 a year earlier, due to a drop in the number of products sold, the Penang-based company said.

A lower first interim dividend of 0.4 sen per share was declared, compared with 0.6 sen in the same quarter last year.

Despite the weaker quarterly results, RGB expressed confidence in its outlook, citing favourable market conditions in key markets such as the Philippines and Cambodia.  

The group cited the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation's forecast of a record 450 billion pesos (RM34.24 billion) in gross gaming revenue for 2025 as a positive indicator for industry growth.

In Cambodia, RGB said it is continuing to expand its footprint, reinforcing its role as a key slot machine distributor and a significant player in the machine concession segment.

Barring any unforeseen circumstances, the group said it expects a satisfactory performance for the full-year.

RGB’s shares closed 3.5 sen or 10.3% lower at 30.5 sen, valuing the group at RM472.2 million. Year to date, the stock has retreated 21.97%.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Berjaya Corp posts fourth straight quarterly loss on weaker food, property segments
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Berjaya Corp Bhd (KL:BJCORP) remained in the red for the fourth consecutive quarter in the third quarter ended March 31, 2025 (3QFY2025), as losses widened on the back of weaker performance in Kenny Rogers Roasters and pre-operating costs related to new overseas ventures.

The diversified group posted a net loss of RM92.34 million in 3QFY2025, compared to a net profit of RM689.92 million in the same period last year. On a quarter-on-quarter basis, the latest net loss widened slightly from RM88.70 million in 2QFY2025.

Quarterly revenue declined 8.49% year-on-year to RM2.54 billion from RM2.78 billion in 3QFY2024, due to a reduced number of Starbucks outlets and the cessation of Papa John’s Pizza operations in the Philippines. No dividend was declared for the quarter under review.

Berjaya Corp said its property division recorded lower earnings following the completion of The Tropika, Bukit Jalil project in the previous financial year, though this was partly offset by stronger local residential sales. The year-ago quarter included contributions from overseas development.

Its non-food business reported higher earnings, mainly driven by the strong performance of HR Owen Plc due to higher sales volume in both the new and used car sectors.

The hospitality segment posted higher revenue on improved occupancy rates, but registered a higher pre-tax loss due to increased operating expenses.

Its services segment reported stronger revenue and earnings, primarily supported by STM Lottery Sdn Bhd, which saw higher ticket sales, driven by a surge in the Supreme Toto 6/58 jackpot and lower prize payouts.

For the nine months of FY2025, Berjaya Corp logged a net loss of RM348.87 million against a net profit of RM580.23 million a year ago, while revenue fell 8% to RM6.97 billion from RM7.58 billion.

Despite current earnings pressure, Berjaya Corp said its domestic business segments are expected to improve, supported by resilient consumer spending and a rebound in tourism-related activities.

The group also anticipates continued growth in its gaming business, underpinned by sustained demand for its Lotto and Digit games.

“Taking account of the aforesaid and barring any unforeseen circumstances, the directors are cautiously optimistic that the group’s business operations will deliver a satisfactory performance for the remaining quarter of the financial year ending June 30, 2025,” the group added.

Shares of Berjaya Corp closed unchanged at 28.5 sen on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation RM1.66 billion. The counter has fallen over 8% year to date.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Farhash Wafa Salvador resigns as 7-Eleven Malaysia chairman
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): 7-Eleven Malaysia Holdings Bhd (KL:SEM) on Friday announced that its non-independent non-executive chairman Datuk Farhash Wafa Salvador has resigned to pursue other interests.

His resignation comes a day after the convenience chain store group held its annual general meeting.

Farhash, 42, joined the board of directors of 7-Eleven Malaysia on Jan 3, 2023.

A former political aide to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and ex-PKR Perak chief, he currently holds directorships in four other public-listed companies, namely MMAG Holdings Bhd (KL:MMAG), Apex Equity Holdings Bhd (KL:APEX), Excel Force MSC Bhd (KL:EFORCE) and Key Alliance Group Bhd (KL:KGROUP).

In April, Farhash became a substantial shareholder in Key Alliance with his purchase of 6.8 million shares in the open market. He owns a 5.546% interest now.

7-Eleven Malaysia’s shares closed up 0.51% to RM1.98 a share on Friday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM2.3 billion.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Spritzer profit up 27% in 1Q as local demand remains robust
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Spritzer Bhd (KL:SPRITZER) enjoyed a 26.68% increase in net profit in its first quarter ended March 31 (1QFY2025), as revenue rose 9.85% amid robust local demand for bottled water products. 

Spritzer booked a net profit of RM19.67 million or 3.37 sen per share in 1QFY2025, an increase from RM15.52 million or 2.43 sen per share in the same quarter a year ago. 

Quarterly revenue rose to RM148.28 million, from RM134.99 million in 1QFY2024.

Looking ahead, Spritzer said in the exchange filing that it will continue to focus on operational excellence, automation and process improvements to navigate ongoing global headwinds and trade uncertainties, with market expansion plans in Singapore.

On the topic of sustainability, Spritzer said it remains central to its operations as ongoing efforts are being made to increase the use of recycled PET in its bottles and explore environmentally friendly packaging alternatives as part of its long-term sustainability goals.

At market close on Friday, shares in Spritzer were down by one sen or 0.61% to RM1.63, valuing the company at RM1.04 billion.
 

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Mah Sing seeks new partners for Bangi data centre project; 1Q net profit rises 10%
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Mah Sing Group Bhd (KL:MAHSING) announced on Friday that it is seeking new partners to develop data centre facilities on 17.55 acres of freehold land in Southville City, Bangi, after talks with Bridge Data Centres lapsed.

The collaboration agreement between Mah Sing and Bridge Data Centres Malaysia V Sdn Bhd (BDC) for a data centre with up to 100-megawatt (MW) power capacity expired following the end of the exclusivity period, according to a filing with Bursa Malaysia.

Mah Sing said its second collaboration with BDC, involving a separate 35.68-acre site planned for a 200MW data centre, is still active, with the agreement valid until Oct 28 this year.

The first collaboration initially began as a joint venture but later evolved into a built-transfer-lease model, where Mah Sing would develop the data centre and lease it to BDC.

Despite agreeing on key commercial terms and securing confirmations of critical infrastructure — including electricity, water and dark fibre — the definitive agreement was not signed before the exclusivity period expired on May 30, said Mah Sing.

Mah Sing said it could not agree to an extension this time as sought by BDC due to the firm's "lack of clear commitment" to finalise the definitive agreement.

1Q net profit rises 10%; on track to meet RM2.65b full-year sales target

Separately, Mah Sing reported a 9.98% year-on-year rise in net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) to RM66.04 million from RM60.05 million, supported by stronger contributions from its property development segment.

Quarterly revenue climbed 16.4% to RM649.69 million from RM558.21 million in 1QFY2024, backed by progressive revenue recognition from ongoing construction projects.

Key earnings contributors during the quarter included M Vertica in Cheras, M Arisa in Sentul, Meridin East, M Tiara and M Minori in Johor Bahru, M Astra in Setapak, M Senyum in Salak Tinggi, M Panora in Rawang and M Nova in Kepong, the company said.

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

Mah Sing said it secured RM1.01 billion in property sales in the first five months of 2025, keeping it on track to meet its full-year sales target of at least RM2.65 billion. The company anticipates stronger financial performance in 2025, supported by unbilled sales of RM2.73 billion and a focus on quick project completions.

The company continued to expand its M-Series portfolio with recent launches such as M Legasi in Semenyih and M Tiara in Johor Bahru, as well as Suria Madani in Taman Desa and Meridin East in Johor Bahru.

Planned launches for the rest of the year include new phases of M Legasi, M Zenya, Meridin East and M Tiara, along with new projects such as M Aurora in Old Klang Road, M Aria in Sentul, Icon City 2 in Petaling Jaya, Southbay City in Penang, and M Tiara 2, Tiara Hills and M Grand Minori in Johor Bahru.

“Prudent capital management has been instrumental in maintaining a robust balance sheet and ample liquidity, resulting in a cash balance of RM790.6 million and a net gearing of 0.17 times as at March 31, 2025,” it said, adding that its financial strength supports land bank expansion, including the recent acquisition of a new parcel in Sentul with a gross development value of RM283 million.

Shares of Mah Sing closed one sen or 0.97% lower at RM1.02 on Friday, valuing the group at RM2.64 billion.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: US ‘bigger bubble’ shift may ignite stocks, says BofA’s Hartnett
data.description:
(May 30): The latest turn by the Trump administration to favour tax cuts and lower tariffs has the potential to kick off another speculative frenzy in markets, according to Bank of America Corp strategists.

The flip in US economic strategy to “we’re going to need a bigger bubble” as the solution to the debt problem could incentivise traders to ditch bonds and pile back into artificial intelligence and crypto trades, wrote the team led Michael Hartnett.

While Hartnett’s view is still that bonds, international stocks and gold are the ideal allocation mix this year, he warned that this all-out bullish scenario poses the biggest risk to his outlook for 2025. US equities and bitcoin have roared back from the depths of the early April sell-off after President Donald Trump backpedalled on his plans for harsh tariffs. Trump has also pressured the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates as a way to spark faster economic growth.

During times of euphoria, the classic relationship between bonds and stocks tends to flip, according to the Bank of America strategists. Bond yields rose in 12 of past 14 asset bubbles, they wrote, adding that “nothing screams bubble more than equities driving nominal/real yields higher.”

The 30-year Treasury yield topped 5% earlier this month after the US was downgraded by Moody’s Ratings and investors worried that higher levels of government debt threaten America’s status as the world’s safe haven. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq 100 has risen almost 10% in May and is on track for its best month since 2023.

By tracing the history of past asset bubbles, the Bank of America strategists estimated about a 30% gain for the Magnificent Seven stocks before a peak. They advised a barbell allocation to the Magnificent Seven and global value stocks as the “best hedge for a bubble.”

Other highlights from the report:

  • Investors withdrew US$9.5 billion (RM40.4 billion) from global equities during the week, the biggest outflow of 2025, the strategists wrote, citing data from EPFR.
  • Weak dollar plays, such as gold, crypto and emerging-market debt and stocks, attracted large inflows.
  • They said 30-year Treasuries yielding 5% are a more attractive bet than the S&P 500.
  • “Higher US allocation requires earnings per share for the benchmark to exceed US$300 via unambiguous global growth acceleration, and AI visibly driving unemployment and productivity higher.”

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: JF Technology completes 80% stake buy in Singapore-based Q3 Probe for RM6m
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): JF Technology Bhd (KL:JFTECH), a manufacturer of high-performance test contacting solutions for global integrated circuit makers, has completed the acquisition of an 80% stake in Singapore-based Q3 Probe Pte Ltd from Q3's US-based parent company Spire Manufacturing Inc for US$1.36 million (RM6 million).

The acquisition was undertaken via its wholly owned subsidiary JF International Sdn Bhd. JF Technology also has an option to acquire the remaining 20% equity interest by Dec 31, 2027, it said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday.

“This acquisition further enhances our vertical integration and elevates the group within the dynamic semiconductor supply chain,” said its managing director Datuk Foong Wei Kuong in a statement.

“Q3’s expertise in front-end wafer testing perfectly complements our test engineering solutions business. This creates a powerful combination that enables us to expand the business on a global scale,” he added.

The acquisition follows a share purchase agreement signed on March 10, which includes a three-year performance-based earn-out structure. Under this structure, JF Technology will pay the vendor US$90,670 (RM401,000) annually — totalling US$272,010 (RM1.2 million) — contingent on Q3 meeting pre-agreed profit targets.

Q3, a probe card manufacturer, specialises in printed circuit board design and assembly, probe cards, and machined parts for industries ranging from semiconductors to LED lighting and broadband systems.

For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2023, Q3 posted a net profit of S$231,000 (RM767,000), according to JF Technology.

Foong said the group plans to set up a production line in Malaysia to manufacture Q3’s products for its base of over 200 multinational clients, reinforcing JF Technology’s position across the entire semiconductor value chain — from front-end to back-end testing.

“By bringing Q3’s capabilities into our fold, we are enhancing our test engineering solutions business and reinforcing our position across the entire semiconductor value chain. We are now the only provider in Malaysia with such a comprehensive probe card product line,” he said.

At market close on Friday, JF Technology shares closed down 2.5 sen or 5.21% at 45.5 sen, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM421.81 million. The stock has declined over 44% year to date.

Read also:
JF Technology in RM6 mil probe card manufacturer acquisition

Edited ByKang Siew Li
